content_en:
And yet another group of scholars take this from the hadith: if one stands to glorify the other person, then that is disliked. But if one stands simply to honor him, then there is no harm in that. AlGhazaalee and Ibn Hajr are among those who hold this view.

In the following words, Ibn Taymiyyah summarized the entire issue:

It was not customary for our pious predecessors, during the lifetime of the Prophet #¢ and his rightly-guided Khaleefahs, to stand whenever they saw the Prophet #%, which is what many people do [today (i.e, they stand whenever they see someone they know)}. In fact, Anas Ibn Maalik said, “They loved no person more than they loved the Prophet #%, yet when they would see him, they would not stand for him, because they knew that he disliked that“! On the other hand, they would sometimes stand for someone who was absent but then arrived, in order to receive and welcome him. Such is related from the Prophet #8, who stood for ‘Ikrimah #. And when Sa’ad Ibn Mu’aadh # was approaching, the Prophet # said to the Ansaar,

“Stand to your chief.” Sa‘ad # was arriving to issue judgment regarding Banu Quraidhah, who agreed to have him judge in their matter. People should accustom themselves to follow our predecessors in what they were upon during the lifetime of the Prophet 2%; after all, they constituted the best generation of this Nation. The best

1] Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (946), and Al-Albaanee said, “sahih.”” 1 Bukhaaree (6262)

Chapter - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another Muslim 103,

speech is Allah’s Speech, and the best guidance is the guidance of Muhammad 2%. No one, therefore, should deviate from the guidance of the best human being or from the guidance of the best generation, choosing instead to follow a guidance that is lesser in goodness. One who holds sway [among people (i.e., a person of authority)} should not approve of his companions if they stand for him upon every common, regular encounter. If people stand to receive someone who is returning from a journey or for someone in similar circumstances, then that is good.

Also, if it is a custom among people to welcome a comer by standing, and if one knows this custom and not the Sunnah whereby he will feel insulted or wronged if people do not stand to meet him, then it is better to stand, because doing so is more likely to keep people on amiable terms and away from hatred and malice. On the other hand, if one knows that the custom of people is in accordance with the Sunnah in this issue, people will not harm him (his ego) by not standing for him.

Tbn Hajr said, “In general, whenever not standing leads to harm or to a person feeling insulted (because people in that society customarily stand for someone who is arriving from elsewhere), [people should stand]. Ibn ‘Abdus-Salaam alluded to that meaning.”

5) Should A Man Kiss His Brother When He Meets Him?

Unlike the situation in our societies today, neither the Companions # nor those who came after them from our pious predecessors would kiss one another when they would meet. Narrations that establish the practice of kissing upon meeting one’s brother are not strong enough to counter the clear hadith that forbids kissing upon the occasion of meeting one’s brother. Al-Albaanee listed the Ahadeeth that establish the practice of one kissing his brother upon meeting him, and he pointed out that they are all defected and thus cannot be used as proofs. Furthermore, if we suppose that they are acceptable, it is not permissible to use them to counter an authentic hadith. And that authentic hadith in this issue is related by Anas Ibn Maalik

, who said, “A man said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, when one of us meets his brother, may he bow for him?’ The Messenger of Allah 2% said, ‘No.’ The man said, ‘Then may he hug him and kiss him?’ The Messenger 2 said,

He then said, ‘Then may he shake his hand?’ The Messenger 2 said,

“Yes, if he wants,’”1)

Although the hadith clearly forbids bowing and kissing for regular, common encounters, it does not forbid hugging someone when he arrives after having been absent for a while or after he returns from a journey, a ruling that is established based on the hadith of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah 2. Jaabir 4 reported that he heard that a companion of the Prophet #, who was in Ash-Sham. at the time, had with him a hadith he himself had not heard. Jaabir 4 wanted to hear it directly from that companion; he later related, “So I bought a camel, and I tied my saddlebag to it, [traveling] for one month, until I reached Ash-Sham, where I encountered ‘Abdullah Ibn Unais # (i.e, the Companio whom he was seeking out). I announced [to a messenger] that Jaabir % is at the door. The messenger then returned and asked, ‘Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah?’ to which I responded, ‘Yes.’ He (‘Abdullah Ibn Unais 4) then came out and hugged me. I said, ‘Ahadeeth reached me that I have not yet heard, and I feared that I or you would die [before I could hear it].’” ‘Abdullah Ibn Unais then said, “I heard the Messenger of Allah # say,

wg Sob tye - GO gf - stad ar Le ‘When Allah will gather His slaves, they will be naked,

' Related by At-Tirmidhee (2728), Ibn Maajah (3702), and others. And AlAlbaanee related it in his Silsilatus-Saheehah (160) (1/248)

Chapter - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another Muslim 105

We asked, ‘What is Buhman?’ He #¢ said,

US 08 Lt AG 3s Biss pe peal yh oe gt

walks tthe Ei ty hs 5h ee

‘They will not have anything with them. Then He (Allah $8) will call out to them with a voice that those far off will hear (and I think he # said, ‘just as those nearby will hear): I am the King. No one from the people of Paradise should enter Paradise while someone from the people of the Hellfire demands restitution from him for some wrong. And no one from the people of the Hellfire should enter the Hellfire while someone from the people of Paradise demands restitution from him for some wrong.’

Isaid, ‘And how is that (how will there be restitution)? For when we will go to Allah, we will be naked and Buhman (without any possession)?’ He #% said,

“[Restitution will be made] with [the exchange of] good and bad deeds,"

Another Related Issue: Scholars disagree about the practice of

1 Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (970), and Al-Albaanee said, “Hasan.”

kissing a person’s hand. One group says that you may do so if you are bestowing honor of the religious kind; however, it is not permissible to do so if you are bestowing honor of the worldly kind. An example of the former is when Abu ‘Ubaidah 4 kissed the hand of ‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab . As for the latter kind, the only exception to the above-mentioned ruling is if you fear punishment or physical harm from a tyrant, for example. One example this group of scholars cites for kissing a person’s hand to confer honor of the religious kind is related from ‘Abdullah, the son of Imam Ahmad. ‘Abdullah said, “I saw many scholars, Fugahaa (scholars of jurisprudence), Muhaddithoon (hadith scholars), people from Banu Haashim, from the Quraish, and from the Ansaar - all kissing him (referring to his father), some on his hand and some on his head.”

Other scholars have disliked the practice of kissing a person’s hand - even if it is to bestow honor of the religious kind - calling it the ‘lesser prostration.’ Sulaimaan Ibn Harb said, “It is the lesser prostration. As for someone intentionally initiating the practice by holding out his hand to people, so that they can kiss it, that is forbidden, no matter who he is; no one disagrees about this ruling. Disagreement [among scholars] arises in this issue when the person who wants to kiss the other person’s hand initiates the practice [by taking hold of the other person’s hand and raising it to his lips].’””

6) When You Greet Someone, It Is Forbidden To Bow To Him Or Perform Prostration Before Him

Anas Ibri Maalik .% said, ‘‘A man said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, when one of us meets his brother, may he bow for him?’ The Messenger of Allah 2 said,

The man said, ‘Then may he hug him and kiss him?’ The Messenger 2% said,

Chapter - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another Muslim 107

He then said, ‘Then may he shake his hand?’ The Messenger # said,

‘Yes, if he wants.”""

The hadith is clear in its categorical prohibition of bowing before another created being; this is because we may only bow, as we do in prayer, to the Creator, Allah 3s. If this is the ruling for bowing, then there is even more cause for the same ruling to apply to prostrating.

Ibn Taymiyyah said, “As for a person bowing when he greets another, doing so is forbidden. It is related in At-Tirmidhee that people asked the Prophet 2% if it is permissible for a man who meets his brother to bow before him? The Prophet #% said, ‘No.’ This is because we are forbidden from bowing and prostrating to anyone but Allah ‘Azza-Wa-Jall.””

Without a doubt, prostration signifies a deeper and higher level of worship than does bowing; the meanings of submission and humility are definitely found in a greater degree in prostrating than they are in bowing. Because of the superlative merits and spiritual qualities - such as complete submission and humility - associated with the act of prostration, the Messenger of Allah 2, in a hadith related by Ibn ‘Abbaas #, said,

“As for the prostration, strive hard to supplicate [while in that

position], for [when you supplicate while prostrating] you are

deserving and worthy of being answered.”" 71

" Related by At-Tirmidhee (2728), Ibn Maajah (3702), and others. And AlAlbaanee related it in his Silsilatus-Saheehah (160) (1/248)

"1 Related by Muslim (479), Ahmad (1903), An-Nassaaee (1045), Abu Daawood (876), Ibn Maajah (3899), and Ad-Daarimee (1325)

Therefore, since prostration involves a great deal of glorification for its object, it is Haram (forbidden) for one to perform it to anyone other than to Allah 38. The proof for this ruling is hadith wherein it is mentioned that when Mu'aadh # returned from Ash-Sham, he performed prostration to the Prophet #2 (not the prostration of worship but that of conferring honor). The Prophet # asked,
content_id:
Ada lagi kelompok ulama yang memahami dari hadith: jika seseorang berdiri untuk mengagungkan orang lain, maka hal itu makruh. Namun jika seseorang berdiri semata-mata untuk menghormatinya, maka tidak ada salahnya. Al-Ghazaalee dan Ibn Hajr termasuk di antara mereka yang berpendapat demikian.

Dalam kata-katanya berikut ini, Ibn Taymiyyah merangkum keseluruhan masalah ini:

Tidaklah menjadi kebiasaan para pendahulu kita yang saleh, semasa hidup Nabi ﷺ dan para Khalifah yang mendapat petunjuk, untuk berdiri setiap kali mereka melihat Nabi ﷺ, sebagaimana yang dilakukan banyak orang [dewasa ini (yakni, mereka berdiri setiap kali melihat seseorang yang mereka kenal)]. Bahkan, Anas Ibn Maalik berkata, "Mereka tidak mencintai siapa pun lebih dari mereka mencintai Nabi ﷺ, namun ketika mereka melihatnya, mereka tidak berdiri untuknya, karena mereka tahu bahwa beliau tidak menyukai hal itu"! Di sisi lain, kadang-kadang mereka berdiri untuk seseorang yang tadinya tidak hadir kemudian tiba, untuk menyambut dan menerimanya. Hal itu diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ, yang berdiri untuk 'Ikrimah رضي الله عنه. Dan ketika Sa'ad Ibn Mu'aadh رضي الله عنه sedang mendekat, Nabi ﷺ bersabda kepada kaum Ansaar,

"Berdirilah untuk pemimpin kalian." Sa'ad رضي الله عنه datang untuk menjatuhkan hukuman terhadap Banu Quraidhah, yang sepakat agar ia yang memutuskan perkara mereka. Orang-orang seharusnya membiasakan diri mengikuti para pendahulu kita dalam apa yang mereka jalani semasa hidup Nabi ﷺ; bagaimanapun, mereka adalah generasi terbaik umat ini.

1] Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree dalam Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (946), dan Al-Albaanee berkata, "sahih." 1 Bukhaaree (6262)

Bab - 4 Adab Bertemu Sesama Muslim 103

Sebaik-baik perkataan adalah Kalam Allah, dan sebaik-baik petunjuk adalah petunjuk Muhammad ﷺ. Oleh karena itu, tidak seorang pun seharusnya menyimpang dari petunjuk manusia terbaik atau dari petunjuk generasi terbaik, lalu memilih mengikuti petunjuk yang lebih rendah kebaikannya. Seorang yang memiliki pengaruh [di antara manusia (yakni, seseorang yang memiliki otoritas)] tidak seharusnya menerima tindakan para pengikutnya jika mereka berdiri untuknya pada setiap pertemuan yang biasa dan rutin. Jika orang-orang berdiri untuk menyambut seseorang yang baru kembali dari perjalanan atau dalam keadaan serupa, maka itu adalah baik.

Juga, jika sudah menjadi kebiasaan di antara manusia untuk menyambut orang yang datang dengan berdiri, dan jika seseorang mengetahui kebiasaan ini tetapi tidak mengetahui Sunnah dalam hal ini sehingga ia akan merasa terhina atau tersakiti jika orang-orang tidak berdiri untuk menyambutnya, maka lebih baik untuk berdiri, karena melakukannya lebih mungkin menjaga hubungan yang harmonis di antara manusia dan menjauhkan dari kebencian dan kedengkian. Di sisi lain, jika seseorang mengetahui bahwa kebiasaan masyarakat sejalan dengan Sunnah dalam masalah ini, orang-orang tidak akan menyakitinya (egonya) dengan tidak berdiri untuknya.

Ibn Hajr berkata, "Secara umum, setiap kali tidak berdiri menimbulkan kerugian atau perasaan terhina pada seseorang (karena masyarakat di sana secara adat berdiri untuk seseorang yang datang dari tempat lain), [orang-orang seharusnya berdiri]. Ibn 'Abdus-Salaam mengisyaratkan makna tersebut."

5) Apakah Seorang Pria Boleh Mencium Saudaranya Ketika Bertemu?

Berbeda dengan keadaan di masyarakat kita saat ini, para Sahabat رضي الله عنهم maupun mereka yang datang setelahnya dari para pendahulu kita yang saleh tidak saling mencium ketika bertemu. Riwayat-riwayat yang menetapkan praktik mencium ketika bertemu saudara tidak cukup kuat untuk menandingi hadith yang jelas melarang mencium saat bertemu saudara. Al-Albaanee menyebutkan Ahadith yang menetapkan praktik seseorang mencium saudaranya ketika bertemu, dan ia menunjukkan bahwa semuanya memiliki cacat sehingga tidak dapat dijadikan hujjah. Lebih jauh lagi, seandainya kita anggap hadith-hadith tersebut dapat diterima, tidaklah diperbolehkan menggunakannya untuk menandingi hadith yang sahih. Dan hadith yang sahih dalam masalah ini diriwayatkan oleh Anas Ibn Maalik

رضي الله عنه, yang berkata, "Seorang pria berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, jika salah seorang di antara kami bertemu saudaranya, bolehkah ia membungkuk untuknya?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, 'Tidak.' Pria itu berkata, 'Lalu bolehkah ia memeluk dan menciumnya?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,

Kemudian ia berkata, 'Lalu bolehkah ia berjabat tangan dengannya?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,

"Ya, jika ia mau."1)

Meskipun hadith tersebut secara jelas melarang membungkuk dan mencium untuk pertemuan biasa yang umum, namun tidak melarang memeluk seseorang ketika ia tiba setelah lama tidak hadir atau setelah ia kembali dari perjalanan, suatu hukum yang ditetapkan berdasarkan hadith Jaabir Ibn 'Abdullah رضي الله عنه. Jaabir رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa ia mendengar seorang sahabat Nabi ﷺ yang saat itu berada di Ash-Sham memiliki suatu hadith yang belum pernah ia dengar sendiri. Jaabir رضي الله عنه ingin mendengarnya langsung dari sahabat tersebut; ia kemudian meriwayatkan, "Maka aku membeli seekor unta, dan aku mengikatkan kantong pelana padanya, [menempuh perjalanan] selama satu bulan, hingga aku sampai di Ash-Sham, di mana aku bertemu 'Abdullah Ibn Unais رضي الله عنه (yakni, sahabat yang ia cari). Aku mengumumkan [kepada seorang utusan] bahwa Jaabir رضي الله عنه ada di pintu. Utusan itu kemudian kembali dan bertanya, 'Jaabir Ibn 'Abdullah?' yang aku jawab, 'Ya.' Ia ('Abdullah Ibn Unais رضي الله عنه) kemudian keluar dan memelukku. Aku berkata, 'Ahadith telah sampai kepadaku yang belum pernah aku dengar, dan aku khawatir aku atau kamu akan meninggal [sebelum aku dapat mendengarnya].'" 'Abdullah Ibn Unais kemudian berkata, "Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,

وَقَالَ Sob tye - GO gf - stad ar Le 'Ketika Allah mengumpulkan para hamba-Nya, mereka akan dalam keadaan telanjang,

' Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee (2728), Ibn Maajah (3702), dan lainnya. Dan Al-Albaanee memuatnya dalam Silsilatus-Saheehah (160) (1/248)

Bab - 4 Adab Bertemu Sesama Muslim 105

Kami bertanya, 'Apa itu Buhman?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda,

US 08 Lt AG 3s Biss pe peal yh oe gt

walks tthe Ei ty hs 5h ee

'Mereka tidak akan memiliki apa pun bersama mereka. Kemudian Dia (Allah سبحانه وتعالى) akan menyeru mereka dengan suara yang akan didengar oleh mereka yang jauh (dan aku kira beliau ﷺ bersabda, "sebagaimana yang akan didengar oleh mereka yang dekat"): Aku adalah Raja. Janganlah seorang pun dari ahli surga masuk surga sementara ada seseorang dari ahli neraka yang menuntut ganti rugi darinya atas suatu kezaliman. Dan janganlah seorang pun dari ahli neraka masuk neraka sementara ada seseorang dari ahli surga yang menuntut ganti rugi darinya atas suatu kezaliman.'

Aku berkata, 'Dan bagaimana hal itu (bagaimana ganti rugi akan dilakukan)? Karena ketika kita menghadap Allah, kita akan telanjang dan dalam keadaan Buhman (tanpa harta apapun)?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda,

"[Ganti rugi akan dilakukan] dengan [pertukaran] amal kebaikan dan keburukan,"

Masalah Terkait Lainnya: Para ulama berselisih pendapat tentang praktik

1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree dalam Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (970), dan Al-Albaanee berkata, "Hasan."

mencium tangan seseorang. Satu kelompok berpendapat bahwa hal itu boleh jika dilakukan untuk memberikan penghormatan yang bersifat keagamaan; namun, tidak diperbolehkan jika dilakukan untuk memberikan penghormatan yang bersifat keduniaan. Contoh yang pertama adalah ketika Abu 'Ubaidah رضي الله عنه mencium tangan 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab رضي الله عنه. Adapun jenis yang kedua, satu-satunya pengecualian dari hukum yang disebutkan di atas adalah jika seseorang takut mendapat hukuman atau bahaya fisik dari seorang tiran, misalnya. Salah satu contoh yang dikutip kelompok ulama ini untuk mencium tangan seseorang guna memberikan penghormatan keagamaan diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah, putra Imam Ahmad. 'Abdullah berkata, "Aku melihat banyak ulama, Fuqahaa (ulama fikih), Muhaddithoon (ulama hadith), orang-orang dari Banu Haashim, dari Quraish, dan dari kaum Ansaar — semuanya menciumnya (merujuk pada ayahnya), sebagian mencium tangannya dan sebagian mencium kepalanya."

Ulama lain tidak menyukai praktik mencium tangan seseorang — meskipun itu untuk memberikan penghormatan keagamaan — menyebutnya sebagai 'sujud yang lebih kecil.' Sulaimaan Ibn Harb berkata, "Itu adalah sujud yang lebih kecil. Adapun seseorang yang dengan sengaja memulai praktik ini dengan mengulurkan tangannya kepada orang-orang agar mereka dapat menciumnya, maka hal itu haram, siapapun orangnya; tidak ada seorang pun yang berbeda pendapat tentang hukum ini. Perbedaan pendapat [di antara para ulama] muncul dalam masalah ini ketika orang yang ingin mencium tangan orang lain yang memulai praktik tersebut [dengan memegang tangan orang lain dan mengangkatnya ke bibirnya]."

6) Ketika Menyapa Seseorang, Dilarang Membungkuk Kepadanya atau Bersujud di Hadapannya

Anas Ibn Maalik رضي الله عنه berkata, "Seorang pria berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, jika salah seorang di antara kami bertemu saudaranya, bolehkah ia membungkuk untuknya?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,

Pria itu berkata, 'Lalu bolehkah ia memeluk dan menciumnya?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,

Bab - 4 Adab Bertemu Sesama Muslim 107

Kemudian ia berkata, 'Lalu bolehkah ia berjabat tangan dengannya?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,

"Ya, jika ia mau."

Hadith tersebut jelas dalam larangan kategorisnya terhadap membungkuk di hadapan makhluk lain; ini karena kita hanya boleh membungkuk, sebagaimana yang kita lakukan dalam salah, kepada Sang Pencipta, Allah سبحانه وتعالى. Jika inilah hukum untuk membungkuk, maka ada alasan yang lebih kuat lagi bagi hukum yang sama untuk berlaku pada bersujud.

Ibn Taymiyyah berkata, "Adapun seseorang yang membungkuk ketika menyapa orang lain, perbuatan itu adalah haram. Diriwayatkan dalam At-Tirmidhee bahwa orang-orang bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ apakah diperbolehkan bagi seorang laki-laki yang bertemu saudaranya untuk membungkuk di hadapannya? Nabi ﷺ bersabda, 'Tidak.' Hal ini karena kita dilarang untuk membungkuk dan bersujud kepada siapa pun selain Allah 'Azza-Wa-Jall.""

Tidak diragukan lagi, sujud menandakan tingkat ibadah yang lebih dalam dan lebih tinggi daripada ruku'; makna ketundukan dan kerendahan hati tentu terdapat dalam derajat yang lebih besar dalam sujud daripada dalam ruku'. Karena keutamaan dan kualitas spiritual yang luar biasa — seperti ketundukan dan kerendahan hati yang sempurna — yang berkaitan dengan perbuatan sujud, Rasulullah ﷺ, dalam sebuah hadith yang diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Abbaas رضي الله عنه, bersabda,

"Adapun sujud, bersungguh-sungguhlah dalam berdu'a [ketika dalam posisi itu], karena [ketika kamu berdu'a saat bersujud] kamu layak dan pantas untuk dikabulkan."" 71

" Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee (2728), Ibn Maajah (3702), dan lainnya. Dan Al-Albaanee meriwayatkannya dalam Silsilatus-Saheehah (160) (1/248)

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (479), Ahmad (1903), An-Nassaaee (1045), Abu Daawood (876), Ibn Maajah (3899), dan Ad-Daarimee (1325)

Oleh karena itu, karena sujud mengandung pengagungan yang sangat besar terhadap objeknya, maka haram bagi seseorang untuk melakukannya kepada siapa pun selain kepada Allah ﷻ. Dalil atas hukum ini adalah hadith yang menyebutkan bahwa ketika Mu'aadh رضي الله عنه kembali dari Ash-Sham, ia bersujud kepada Nabi ﷺ (bukan sujud ibadah melainkan sujud penghormatan). Nabi ﷺ bertanya,
content_ar: And yet another group of العلماء take this from the الحديث: إذا one stands to glorify the other person, then that is disliked. But إذا one stands simply to الشرف him, then there is no harm in that. AlGhazaalee and Ibn Hajr are among those who hold this view. In the following words, ابن تيمية summarized the entire issue: It was not customary for our pious predecessors, خلال the lifetime of النبي ﷺ #¢ and his rightly-guided Khaleefahs, to stand كلما they saw النبي ﷺ #%, which is what many people do [today (i.e, they stand كلما they see someone they know)}. In fact, أنس Ibn Maalik said, “They loved no person more than they loved النبي ﷺ #%, yet عندما they would see him, they would not stand for him, لأن they knew that he disliked that“! من ناحية أخرى, they would sometimes stand for someone who was absent but then arrived, من أجل receive and welcome him. Such is related from النبي ﷺ #8, who stood for ‘Ikrimah #. And عندما Sa’ad Ibn Mu’aadh # was approaching, النبي ﷺ # said to the Ansaar, “Stand to your chief.” Sa‘ad # was arriving to issue judgment فيما يخص Banu Quraidhah, who agreed to have him judge in their matter. People should accustom themselves للاتباع our predecessors in what they were upon خلال the lifetime of النبي ﷺ 2%; بعد all, they constituted the best generation of this Nation. The best 1] Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (946), and Al-Albaanee said, “صحيح.”” 1 Bukhaaree (6262) الباب - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another مسلم 103, speech is الله’s Speech, and the best الهداية is the الهداية of محمد ﷺ 2%. No one, لذلك, should deviate from the الهداية of the best human being or from the الهداية of the best generation, choosing instead للاتباع a الهداية that is lesser in goodness. One who holds sway [among people (i.e., a person of authority)} should not approve of his الصحابة إذا they stand for him upon every common, regular encounter. إذا people stand to receive someone who is returning from a journey or for someone in similar circumstances, then that is الخير. أيضاً, إذا it is a custom among people to welcome a comer by standing, and إذا one knows this custom and not the السنة whereby he will feel insulted or wronged إذا people do not stand to meet him, then it is better to stand, لأن doing لذا is more likely to keep people on amiable terms and away from hatred and malice. من ناحية أخرى, إذا one knows that the custom of people is in accordance with the السنة in this issue, people will not harm him (his ego) by not standing for him. Tbn Hajr said, “In general, كلما not standing leads to harm or to a person feeling insulted (لأن people in that المجتمع customarily stand for someone who is arriving from elsewhere), [people should stand]. Ibn ‘Abdus-Salaam alluded to that meaning.” 5) Should A Man Kiss His الأخ عندما He Meets Him? Unlike the situation in our societies today, neither the الصحابة # nor those who came بعد them from our pious predecessors would kiss بعضهم بعضاً عندما they would meet. Narrations that establish the practice of kissing upon meeting one’s الأخ are not strong enough to counter the clear الحديث that forbids kissing upon the occasion of meeting one’s الأخ. Al-Albaanee listed the Ahadeeth that establish the practice of one kissing his الأخ upon meeting him, and أشار إلى that they are all defected and وهكذا cannot be used as proofs. علاوة على ذلك, إذا we suppose that they are acceptable, لا يجوز to use them to counter an authentic الحديث. And that authentic الحديث in this issue is related by أنس Ibn Maalik , who said, “A man said, ‘O Messenger of الله, عندما one of us meets his الأخ, may he bow for him?’ الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, ‘No.’ The man said, ‘Then may he hug him and kiss him?’ الرسول ﷺ 2 said, He then said, ‘Then may he shake his hand?’ الرسول ﷺ 2 said, “Yes, إذا he wants,’”1) وإن كان the الحديث clearly forbids bowing and kissing for regular, common encounters, it does not forbid hugging someone عندما he arrives بعد having been absent for a بينما or بعد he returns from a journey, a ruling that is established based on the الحديث of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah 2. Jaabir 4 reported that he heard that a companion of النبي ﷺ #, who was in Ash-Sham. at the time, had with him a الحديث he himself had not heard. Jaabir 4 wanted to hear it directly from that companion; he later related, “لذا I bought a camel, and I tied my saddlebag to it, [traveling] for one month, حتى I reached Ash-Sham, where I encountered ‘Abdullah Ibn Unais # (i.e, the Companio whom he was seeking out). I announced [to a messenger] that Jaabir % is at the door. الرسول ﷺ then returned and asked, ‘Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah?’ to which I responded, ‘Yes.’ He (‘Abdullah Ibn Unais 4) then came out and hugged me. I said, ‘Ahadeeth reached me that I have not yet heard, and I feared that I or you would die [قبل I could hear it].’” ‘Abdullah Ibn Unais then said, “I heard الرسول ﷺ of الله # say, wg Sob tye - GO gf - stad ar Le ‘عندما الله will gather His slaves, they will be naked, ' Related by At-Tirmidhee (2728), Ibn Maajah (3702), and others. And AlAlbaanee related it in his Silsilatus-Saheehah (160) (1/248) الباب - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another مسلم 105 We asked, ‘What is Buhman?’ He #¢ said, US 08 Lt AG 3s Biss pe peal yh oe gt walks tthe Ei ty hs 5h ee ‘They will not have anything with them. Then He (الله $8) will call out to them with a voice that those far off will hear (and I think he # said, ‘just as those nearby will hear): I am the King. No one from the people of الجنة should enter الجنة بينما someone from the people of the النار demands restitution from him for some الخطأ. And no one from the people of the النار should enter the النار بينما someone from the people of الجنة demands restitution from him for some الخطأ.’ Isaid, ‘And how is that (how will there be restitution)? For عندما we will go to الله, we will be naked and Buhman (without any possession)?’ He #% said, “[Restitution will be made] with [the exchange of] الخير and bad deeds," Another Related Issue: العلماء disagree about the practice of 1 Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (970), and Al-Albaanee said, “حسن.” kissing a person’s hand. One group says that you may do لذا إذا you are bestowing الشرف of the religious kind; غير أن, لا يجوز to do لذا إذا you are bestowing الشرف of the worldly kind. An example of the former is عندما Abu ‘Ubaidah 4 kissed the hand of ‘عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab . أما the latter kind, the only exception to the above-mentioned ruling is إذا you الخوف العقاب or physical harm from a tyrant, على سبيل المثال. One example this group of العلماء cites for kissing a person’s hand to confer الشرف of the religious kind is related from ‘Abdullah, the son of الإمام أحمد. ‘Abdullah said, “I saw many العلماء, Fugahaa (العلماء of jurisprudence), Muhaddithoon (الحديث العلماء), people from Banu Haashim, from the Quraish, and from the Ansaar - all kissing him (referring to his الأب), some on his hand and some on his head.” Other العلماء have disliked the practice of kissing a person’s hand - even إذا it is to bestow الشرف of the religious kind - calling it the ‘lesser prostration.’ Sulaimaan Ibn Harb said, “It is the lesser prostration. أما someone intentionally initiating the practice by holding out his hand to people, لذا that they can kiss it, that is forbidden, no matter who he is; no one disagrees about this ruling. Disagreement [among العلماء] arises in this issue عندما the person who wants to kiss the other person’s hand initiates the practice [by taking hold of the other person’s hand and raising it to his lips].’”” 6) عندما You Greet Someone, يُحرَّم To Bow To Him Or Perform Prostration قبل Him أنس Ibri Maalik .% said, ‘‘A man said, ‘O Messenger of الله, عندما one of us meets his الأخ, may he bow for him?’ الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, The man said, ‘Then may he hug him and kiss him?’ الرسول ﷺ 2% said, الباب - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another مسلم 107 He then said, ‘Then may he shake his hand?’ الرسول ﷺ # said, ‘Yes, إذا he wants.”"" The الحديث is clear in its categorical prohibition of bowing قبل another created being; this is لأن we may only bow, as we do in الصلاة, to the Creator, الله 3s. إذا this is the ruling for bowing, then there is even more cause for the same ruling to apply to prostrating. ابن تيمية said, “أما a person bowing عندما he greets another, doing لذا is forbidden. It is related in At-Tirmidhee that people asked النبي ﷺ 2% إذا يجوز for a man who meets his الأخ to bow قبل him? النبي ﷺ #% said, ‘No.’ This is لأن we are forbidden from bowing and prostrating to anyone but الله ‘Azza-Wa-Jall.”” Without a doubt, prostration signifies a deeper and higher level of worship than does bowing; the meanings of submission and التواضع are definitely found in a greater degree in prostrating than they are in bowing. لأن of the superlative merits and spiritual qualities - مثل complete submission and التواضع - associated with the act of prostration, الرسول ﷺ of الله 2, in a الحديث related by Ibn ‘Abbaas #, said, “أما the prostration, strive hard to supplicate [بينما in that position], for [عندما you supplicate بينما prostrating] you are deserving and worthy of being answered.”" 71 " Related by At-Tirmidhee (2728), Ibn Maajah (3702), and others. And AlAlbaanee related it in his Silsilatus-Saheehah (160) (1/248) "1 Related by مسلم (479), أحمد (1903), An-Nassaaee (1045), Abu Daawood (876), Ibn Maajah (3899), and Ad-Daarimee (1325) لذلك, بما أن prostration involves a great deal of glorification for its object, it is الحرام (forbidden) for one to perform it to anyone other than to الله 38.
